title: Compress PDF Free | Everything PDF
metaDescription: Shrink your PDF's file size for free with PDF compression. Your files are processed right in your browser instead of on a server, so they stay safe.
subtitle: Shrink your file size right on your device — free, no install, no sign-up.
intro: Bumping up against an email attachment cap or an upload-site size limit? Shrink your PDF down. It works by re-rendering each page as an image and recompressing it to fit your chosen quality, so it's especially effective on scans and photo-heavy PDFs. Your files are never uploaded to a server — everything happens right in your browser.
steps: Upload the PDF you want to shrink.
Pick a quality level: Low, Medium, or High. Lower means smaller.
Compare the new size against the original.
Save the compressed PDF to your device.
security: All compression happens inside your browser, and files are never uploaded to a server — so sensitive documents like contracts, résumés, and official paperwork never leave your hands. Unlike foreign tools that upload your file and delete it after a while, we never upload it in the first place.

faq.q: Will a text-only PDF get smaller too?
faq.a: Text-only documents shrink very little — and can even grow larger. This tool works by re-rendering pages as images and compressing them, so it isn't a good fit for lightweight, text-only PDFs. It shines on scans and photo-heavy PDFs.

faq.q: Can I still select or search the text after compressing?
faq.a: After compression, text becomes an image, so selecting, copying, or searching the body text may be difficult. If you need the text to stay live, keep a copy of the original. What you see on the page looks exactly the same.

faq.q: How do I choose between Low, Medium, and High quality?
faq.a: Pick Low to shrink as much as possible, or High to keep things crisp and clear. Medium sits in between. If you're hitting an email attachment limit, start with Low; if the document will be printed, High is the safe bet.

faq.q: Can I see how much it'll shrink beforehand?
faq.a: It varies by document. Scanned and photo PDFs can drop to less than half their size, while text-heavy documents may barely shrink or even grow. After compressing, compare the size to the original — and if you're not happy with the result, change the quality and try again.

faq.q: Do I need to sign up or install software?
faq.a: No. You can start using it right away with no account and no install. It's completely free, adds no watermark, and has no limit on file count or size.

extraSections.heading: Great for moments like these
extraSections.body: When a scanned contract or ID copy is too big for an upload site's size cap, when you need to email a photo-heavy deck or portfolio, or when a scanned report is too heavy to send over messenger. On the flip side, a lightweight text-only PDF won't shrink much, so you may be better off sending the original as is.
